title: Tanacetum germanicopolitanum
intro: Tanacetum germanicopolitanum, also known as German Tanacetum, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, to treat conditions such as fever, headaches, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum germanicopolitanum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Tanacetum germanicopolitanum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in the production of essential oils and phytomedicines.

The genus Tanacetum is a group of flowering plants known for their daisy-like flowers and often referred to as tansy.
